modules.title: 🌙 CRT & SMC Week 1: The Core Mechanics of Candle Range Theory (CRT)
modules.description: Welcome to Track 3. Tonight we strip away generic charting patterns and dissect the internal anatomy of a single candle range to understand institutional timing.

fields.name: 📐 The Power of Three (AMD)
fields.value:
Every major instructional candle or structural session undergoes three distinct phase sequences:
1. **Accumulation:** Price tightly consolidates, building up orders.
2. **Manipulation:** Price aggressively sweeps liquidity past one side of the consolidation, trapping retail breakouts.
3. **Distribution:** Price violently reverses and expands in the true intended direction.

fields.name: 📏 Fractional Range Divisions
fields.value: An established candle range is divided mathematically. The absolute center point of the body is the **Equilibrium (0.5)**. The upper 25% represents the **Premium Zone** (ideal for shorting), and the lower 25% represents the **Discount Zone** (ideal for buying).

modules.title: ☀️ CRT & SMC Week 1 Mid-Week Drill: Mapping Daily AMD Ranges
modules.description: Today we take the AMD theory and map it live against historical Daily candles to find recurring retail traps.

fields.name: 🛠️ The Midnight Midnight Range Setup
fields.value: Open TradingView on **EUR/USD** or **GBP/USD**. Mark a vertical line at 00:00 EST (New York Midnight). The price action from midnight to London Open (03:00 EST) is your **Accumulation Range**. Watch how the London open frequently manipulates below this range before distributing higher.

fields.name: 📊 Practical Execution Log
fields.value: Identify the last 5 daily candles. Highlight the lower wick of those candles. Note how often that wick was printed during the London session open window. Document your findings cleanly.

modules.title: 🌙 CRT & SMC Week 2: Advanced Market Structure Shifts (MSS) vs. BOS
modules.description: Tonight we look at the exact difference between a trend continuation and an institutional trend reversal using clean SMC logic.

fields.name: 🔄 BOS vs. MSS Explained
fields.value:
• **Break of Structure (BOS):** Price breaks past a swing high/low in the direction of the *current* trend, validating continuation.
• **Market Structure Shift (MSS):** Price aggressively closes past the final structural swing low/high that created the peak, signaling an official trend *reversal*.

fields.name: 💥 Displacement Validation
fields.value: A true MSS requires **Displacement**—a massive, high-volume candle that cuts cleanly through the structural level with real institutional power. Tiny, slow candles or simple wick pierces are fakeouts.

modules.title: ☀️ CRT & SMC Week 2 Mid-Week Drill: Refining Reversal Signatures
modules.description: Today we isolate structural shifts on the lower execution timeframes inside macro higher-timeframe zones.

fields.name: 🤖 Binary Confirmation Ruleset
fields.value:
1. Price reaches a major 4-Hour structural Supply Zone.
2. Drop down to the 15-Minute chart.
3. Wait for an explicit 15M Market Structure Shift with high candle displacement. This confirms institutional sponsorship.

fields.name: 📐 Mathematical Balance Guardrail
fields.value: Measure the displacement move using a Fibonacci tool. The displacement must create an internal **Fair Value Gap (FVG)** located strictly above the Equilibrium (0.5) line for a high-probability sell entry.

modules.title: 🌙 CRT & SMC Week 3: Internal Liquidity Mechanics & Inducement
modules.description: To be a profitable trader, you must identify where liquidity sits. If you cannot find the liquidity in the market, your stop-loss *is* the liquidity.

fields.name: 🧲 Inducement (IDM) Demystified
fields.value: Inducement is a minor internal structure high/low designed to trap eager retail traders into buying or selling too early. Smart Money deliberately prints these traps right before hitting the actual institutional Order Block.

fields.name: 🛒 Liquidity Pool Classifications
fields.value:
• **BSL (Buy-Side Liquidity):** Clustered above clean double tops and swing highs.
• **SSL (Sell-Side Liquidity):** Clustered below clean double bottoms and swing lows.

modules.title: ☀️ CRT & SMC Week 3 Mid-Week Drill: Mapping Sweeps and Targets
modules.description: Today we practice looking at the chart purely as an internal game of hunting and capturing liquidity blocks.

fields.name: 🤖 Mechanical Execution Blueprint
fields.value:
1. Identify an obvious pool of Sell-Side Liquidity (SSL) on the 1-Hour chart.
2. Monitor price as it aggressively sweeps past that low.
3. Look for a 5-Minute structural shift immediately following the sweep to confirm the trap is set.

fields.name: 📝 Database Logging Requirement
fields.value: Log the time duration between the initial sweep and the corresponding reversal pattern. Push this baseline entry criteria into your tracking files to monitor setup efficiency.

modules.title: 🌙 CRT & SMC Week 4: The Unified Confluence Model
modules.description: Tonight we stitch Candle Range Theory, Market Structure Shifts, and Liquidity Pools into a single, cohesive mechanical execution strategy.

fields.name: ⚔️ The 4-Step Execution Blueprint
fields.value:
1. **Time & Filter:** Wait for the New York Midnight Accumulation range to establish.
2. **The Sweep:** Watch the London Session manipulate outside the range to hunt internal liquidity pools.
3. **The Reversal Shift:** Wait for a sharp Market Structure Shift with an open Fair Value Gap on the lower timeframe.
4. **The Safe Entry:** Execute your position strictly on the mitigation/retest of that newly formed gap.

modules.title: ☀️ CRT & SMC Week 4 Mid-Week Drill: Building the Automated Blueprint
modules.description: Our final module. We finalize our technical parameters and ensure our tracking systems are ready for forward-testing.

fields.name: 🔧 Database State Optimization
fields.value: Ensure your signal recording setups in `signals.db` include separate tracking flags for `Midnight_Accumulation_High`, `London_Sweep_True`, and `MSS_Displacement_Pips`. This structural data formatting sets up flawless parameters for long-term quantitative system queries.
